text: The dimming of a star holds a secret about its planets. When a planet transits—crosses in front of its star—it causes a temporary dip in brightness. But how much it dims reveals the planet's size compared to the star. A larger planet blocks more light, creating a deeper transit signal. This method helps astronomers estimate sizes without ever visiting those distant worlds. Curious about more cosmic mysteries? Like and follow for more insights!
